prose:
Water (H₂O) frontier-orbital analysis performed using DFT with tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional and basis set) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Input geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz (single-point, not optimized). SCF converged in 7 cycles. 

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV** (Koopmans-style frontier-orbital energy difference). HOMO energy: −8.6037183 eV (index 4, doubly occupied). LUMO energy: +0.33695037 eV (index 5, virtual). Total electronic energy: −76.462901 Hartree. All integrity checks passed (energy finite, SCF converged, schema valid). 

Full result includes 3 occupied and 3 virtual orbitals on each side of the gap, plus Koopmans-derived properties (vertical ionization potential, electron affinity, electronegativity, chemical hardness, and electrophilicity index). Method: B3LYP/def2-TZVP with exact (non-density-fitted) four-center integrals.</prose>
</invoke>
